title: Add queryable block/item registry with fuzzy matching and aliases
description:
The agent needs a queryable block/item registry so it can look up Minecraft block IDs when the LLM doesn't know the exact name. Examples from testing:

- "place a dirt block" — LLM might say "dirt" but the actual block ID might be "minecraft:dirt"
- "craft a wooden pickaxe" — LLM says "wooden_pickaxe" but it might be "wooden_pickaxe" in newer versions
- Creative inventory items might have different IDs than survival drops

The registry should:
1. Be queryable by partial/fuzzy name matching
2. Include common aliases (e.g., "wood" → "oak_log", "stone brick" → "stone_bricks")
3. Integrate with the MemorySmith item registry (Data/Pages/item-registry/)
4. Be available to both the LLM prompt (for command generation) and the planner (for action validation)
5. Support Minecraft version-specific IDs where needed

Related to TSK-0282 (normalize item IDs) and the existing MemorySmithItemRegistry in Agent.Memory.
